intake.risk_posture: Preserve current /builder-v2 behavior and all /builder behavior; prefer characterized root-fix slices; stop before product/design/DnD feature decisions.
intake.harness_policy: Goal packet and focused tests allowed; no AGENTS.md, lint, dependencies, plugin skills, or remote state without routed maintenance and explicit approval.
allowed_areas: narrowly related src/features/builder/types.ts when selected by Judge
narrowly related src/features/builder/team-state.ts when selected by Judge
read-only references to V1 builder tests/helpers
validation_commands: targeted npx eslint over touched files
focused shared builder helper tests when shared helpers/types are touched
intake.stop_condition: Stop when the active tranche lands and is verified, a product/design decision is needed, verification fails twice on the same blocker, same-concept expansion crosses protected scope, or work drifts into mobile redesign, teams-list shape, or DnD feature implementation.

focus_areas.name: TypeScript/model invariants
focus_areas.evidence: TypeScript scout found broad ActiveSelection, broad wheel indexes, invalid editing-mode combinations, and storage envelope cast concerns.
focus_areas.next: Queue selection/editing-mode and wheel-index candidates; persistence parsing tracked but outside first tranche unless selected.

focus_areas.name: React model/hook boundaries
focus_areas.evidence: React and complexity scouts found useBuilderV2Model.ts still owns persistence, projections, picker queries, assignments, transfers, dialogs, and import/export wiring.
focus_areas.next: W1/W2 characterized assignment behavior and split loadout command resolution out of the hook; C3/C6 remain queued for editing-mode and picker-query boundaries.

focus_areas.name: Tailwind/CSS contracts
focus_areas.evidence: CSS scout found duplicated rail/button styling, incomplete focus/motion contracts, clipping risks, and picker grid sizing split across contexts.
focus_areas.next: Queue CSS hardening candidates after core command boundary or as a separate Worker slice.

focus_areas.name: UI/a11y hardening
focus_areas.evidence: UI/a11y scout found duplicate picker IDs, incomplete picker tile accessible names, adaptive drawer focus issues, reduced-motion gaps, and fixed picker grid overflow risk.
focus_areas.next: Queue shared picker a11y and adaptive focus candidates.

focus_areas.name: Complexity/root-fix
focus_areas.evidence: Complexity audit reports useBuilderV2Model.ts at about 1980 LOC, max hook body about 1652 lines, 341 rough branches, 11 effects. Complexity scout prioritizes loadout command resolver and explicit team-state results.
focus_areas.next: W1/W2 landed the characterization-first loadout command resolver tranche. Next recommended slice is C3 selection/editing-mode boundary, then C6 picker query modules.

focus_areas.name: DnD future-readiness
focus_areas.evidence: Scouts agree current assignment handlers and slot DTO shape would multiply future dnd-kit/drop/drag-ghost handler paths.
focus_areas.next: Keep DnD implementation out until command/target boundaries are ready.

focus_areas.name: Zustand/Immer state strategy
focus_areas.evidence: User asked whether there is a point; current evidence says state is complex, but no scout proved a dependency/state-library shift is the root fix.
focus_areas.next: Queue as review-only candidate; dependency/state-library changes require $refactor-dependencies and explicit approval.

candidates.title: Assignment behavior characterization
candidates.evidence: Complexity scout recommended characterization before loadout command refactor: duplicate transfer, same-team wheel swap, cross-team wheel transfer, covenant swap/noop, posse transfer, quick-lineup advancement, selection after assignment.
candidates.next: Implemented by W1: added focused assignment characterization tests for wheel/covenant swaps, support-slot wheel transfer gating, assignment violations, and awakener violation picker-tab preservation.

candidates.title: Builder V2 loadout command resolver
candidates.evidence: assignAwakener, assignWheel, assignCovenant, and assignPosse repeat target resolution, transfer branching, mutation, target clearing, picker tab setting, quick-lineup advancement, and selection restoration.
candidates.next: Implemented by W2: extracted pure Builder V2 loadout command resolution into builder-v2-loadout-commands.ts and left useBuilderV2Model.ts applying resolved outcomes.

candidates.title: V2 selection/editing-mode boundary
candidates.evidence: activeSelection, activeTeamTarget, and pickerTab are independent fields for one editing mode and can drift into impossible states; selection can reference non-existent slots.
candidates.next: Implemented by W3: added a local editing-target helper and routed common V2 selection/focus/apply paths through it while preserving same-batch toggle and explicit picker-tab behavior.

candidates.title: Shared WheelSlotIndex type
candidates.evidence: Shared QuickLineupStep, ActiveSelection, and WheelUsageLocation use number; V2 clamps malformed values to 1.
candidates.next: Implemented by W8: introduced shared WheelSlotIndex plus narrow parse/normalize helpers and routed V1/V2 trusted wheel-selection, usage-index, and transfer/drag data surfaces through the typed boundary while preserving team-state runtime guards.

candidates.title: Explicit team-state operation result
candidates.evidence: Callers use result.nextSlots === activeTeamSlots as noop control flow.
candidates.next: Good root-fix candidate after command-boundary characterization; may need adapter to avoid V1 churn.

candidates.title: Pure picker option query modules
candidates.evidence: Four picker option pipelines are large useMemo blocks coupled to hook state and hard to test directly.
candidates.next: Implemented by W4 with builder-v2-picker-options.ts; hook retains inactive-tab gates and pure tests cover status/recommendation contracts.

candidates.title: Stable action groups and adaptive callback churn
candidates.evidence: Desktop wraps assignment callbacks with useStableEvent; adaptive callbacks close over the entire model.
candidates.next: Implemented by W7 with a shared Builder V2 useStableEvent hook and stable adaptive picker open/assignment callbacks.

candidates.title: Slot interaction descriptors for drag ghosts
candidates.evidence: BuilderV2SlotView is a render DTO containing labels/assets/selection, not stable interaction descriptors.
candidates.next: Queue as DnD-prep after C2/C3. Do not implement DnD here.

candidates.title: Shared picker a11y hardening
candidates.evidence: Shared picker content reuses fixed DOM IDs across desktop and adaptive instances; tile aria-labels omit blocked/unowned/recommended/active statuses.
candidates.next: Implemented by W5 with instance-safe picker IDs, same-instance tab focus, and richer tile accessible names.

candidates.title: Adaptive drawer focus containment/restore
candidates.evidence: Assignment close path suppresses focus restore; focus trap lacks outside-activeElement fallback.
candidates.next: Implemented by W6 with opener-aware focus restore and document-level Tab containment for the adaptive picker drawer.

candidates.title: Builder V2 motion/focus CSS contract
candidates.evidence: Reduced motion only covers drawer animations; focus styles are partly global and partly bespoke.
candidates.next: Implemented by W9: centralized Builder V2 focus ring tokens and added a scoped reduced-motion contract for picker/backdrop/card-art motion.

candidates.title: Rail/button CSS dedupe
candidates.evidence: Desktop/adaptive rail buttons and button recipes duplicate nearly identical styles.
candidates.next: Implemented by W11: deduped desktop/adaptive rail button and primary picker/lineup button CSS through shared selectors without JSX or layout-shape changes.

candidates.title: Clipping and drag overlay CSS boundary
candidates.evidence: .builder-v2-panel and cards use clipping for both panel containment and art masks, which can clip future drag ghosts/focus halos.
candidates.next: Queue before DnD implementation; do not add DnD here.

candidates.title: Picker grid sizing contract
candidates.evidence: Desktop picker uses fixed 4-column tracks while adaptive/mobile override via ancestor selectors.
candidates.next: Implemented by W9: replaced fixed desktop picker result columns and duplicated adaptive/mobile overrides with a shared picker grid sizing contract.

candidates.title: Persistence envelope parser
candidates.evidence: JSON.parse(raw) as PersistedBuilderEnvelope casts before envelope validation.
candidates.next: Implemented by W10: added a runtime envelope parser for current and legacy builder draft storage so JSON.parse output is narrowed before payload validation.

candidates.title: Zustand/Immer state strategy review
candidates.evidence: Current evidence does not prove a state-library/dependency shift is needed.
candidates.next: Judge/review-only candidate after C2/C3; dependency changes require approval.

maintenance.title: Repeated broad-model callback churn pattern
maintenance.next: Consider after C2/C7 whether a local lesson or lintable review rule would prevent wide model dependencies in Builder V2 layouts.

maintenance.title: CSS focus/motion contract drift
maintenance.next: Consider after C11 whether a documented Builder V2 CSS contract or lint/test guard is useful.

tasks.title: Map Builder V2 hardening candidates

tasks.title: Choose first hardening Worker slice
allowed_files: read-only direct shared builder helpers/types if comparing candidates
tasks.expected_output: A single approved Worker slice with allowed files, invariants, validation, rollback, and root-fix decision. Likely choose C1 characterization first or combined C1/C2 only if safe.

evidence: Selected C1 Assignment behavior characterization as the first Worker slice.

Root-fix decision: C2 command extraction is the right architectural direction, but C1 must land first because assignment behavior includes transfer, noop, quick-lineup, picker-close, and selection-restoration semantics that are easy to drift.
Rejected a combined C1/C2 first slice because it would mix test discovery with implementation changes in the highest-risk Builder V2 model paths.
validation: No product code changed during J1.
Approved next Worker W1 with tests-only allowed files, behavior-preserving invariants, validation commands, and rollback.

tasks.expected_output: Focused characterization tests for current Builder V2 assignment semantics: duplicate/transfer paths, same-team wheel swaps, cross-team wheel transfer, covenant swap/noop behavior, posse transfer, quick-lineup advancement, and active selection/picker state after assignment. No product behavior changes.
invariants: Do not change product source in W1.
Tests should assert current observed behavior, not desired redesign behavior.
If an expected behavior is ambiguous or contradicts existing UI/test evidence, stop and record a needs_judge note instead of inventing product behavior.
Do not add DnD, mobile layout, teams-list, import/export, or visual-polish scope.

tasks.title: Harden adaptive picker focus restore and containment
tasks.expected_output: Preserve adaptive picker visuals and assignment-close behavior while making focus restore target-aware for slot/wheel/covenant/open-button entry points and making the dialog focus trap recover if focus starts outside the dialog.
invariants: Do not change drawer markup classes, layout, backdrop behavior, visual styling, or mobile layout.
Search input still receives focus on open and picker tab changes.
Escape, close button, and backdrop click still close the adaptive picker.
Escape/close focus restore should target the control that opened the drawer when possible.
Successful assignment paths that intentionally call closePicker(false) must not start restoring focus.
Focus trap should keep Tab and Shift+Tab inside the dialog, including when focus has drifted outside while open.
